sourcesContent:
import * as React from 'react'; import { Link, useParams } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Clock, BookOpen, Users, Star, Play, Award, Target, User, Building, FileText, Calendar, Shield, Scale, Eye, Lock } from 'lucide-react'; const ScenarioDetail: React.FC = () => { const { id } = useParams<{ id: string }>(); // Actual scenario data based on the PDF content - presented as a mystery to solve const scenario = { _id: '1', title: 'The Inventory That Changed Everything', subtitle: 'First-Time Bail Application – Theft Offense', description: 'A warehouse supervisor is accused of theft after valuable electronics go missing from his workplace. As his defense attorney, you must investigate the case, interview the client, research relevant laws, and argue for bail in court. The truth is hidden in the details - can you uncover what really happened?', caseOverview: `Case File: The Inventory Mystery Client: Rajesh Kumar, 32 Occupation: Warehouse Supervisor Charge: Theft of electronic devices valued at ₹2,50,000 Current Status: In police custody, bail hearing scheduled for tomorrow Initial Briefing: On October 15, 2025, Rajesh Kumar conducted his monthly inventory check at a mid-sized electronics distributor in Bangalore. To his shock, he discovered that 50 high-end smartphones worth ₹2,50,000 were missing from the records. His manager, Mr. Suresh, immediately contacted the police, and Rajesh was taken into custody. Rajesh maintains his innocence, claiming he followed all proper procedures and locked the warehouse correctly. Security footage shows him leaving at 7:30 p.m., but there's a gap in recording from 9:00 p.m. to 11:00 p.m. Your Role: As Rajesh's defense attorney, you must: 1. Conduct a thorough client interview to gather all facts 2. Investigate potential alternative explanations 3. Research relevant bail laws and precedents 4. Prepare compelling arguments for the bail hearing 5. Uncover the truth behind the missing inventory The evidence is there - you just need to find it.`, practiceArea: 'Criminal Law', difficulty: 'Intermediate', estimatedTime: 30, rating: 4.7, reviewCount: 32, price: 0, // Free scenario tags: ['bail application', 'client interviewing', 'legal research', 'oral advocacy', 'investigation', 'evidence analysis'], status: 'published', createdAt: '2025-01-15T10:30:00Z', updatedAt: '2025-01-20T14:45:00Z', creator: { username: 'LegalExpert2025', name: 'Legal Training Institute' }, learningObjectives: [ 'Conduct an effective client interview to gather critical facts', 'Analyze evidence and identify gaps in the prosecution\'s case', 'Research relevant bail laws and precedents under CrPC', 'Develop critical thinking and legal reasoning skills', 'Prepare compelling arguments for bail grant', 'Present oral arguments in a mock bail hearing', 'Understand the importance of thorough investigation in building a defense' ], courtType: 'Magistrate Court', parties: [ { role: 'Defendant', name: 'Rajesh Kumar', description: 'Warehouse supervisor accused of theft' }, { role: 'Defense Attorney', name: 'You', description: 'Assigned to represent the defendant' }, { role: 'Prosecutor', name: 'State Prosecutor', description: 'Representing the state in the criminal case' }, { role: 'Witness', name: 'Mr. Suresh', description: 'Warehouse manager who reported the incident' }, { role: 'Potential Witness', name: 'Ravi', description: 'Assistant with access to the warehouse' } ], skillFocus: [ 'Client Interviewing', 'Legal Research', 'Evidence Analysis', 'Oral Advocacy', 'Case Preparation', 'Critical Thinking' ], duration: '20-30 minutes', assessmentCriteria: [ 'Thoroughness of client interview', 'Quality of legal research', 'Identification of evidence gaps', 'Strength of arguments presented', 'Oral advocacy skills', 'Professional courtroom demeanor' ], investigationPoints: [ 'Security camera footage analysis', 'Warehouse key access records', 'Employee background checks', 'Financial records of suspects', 'Physical evidence at the scene', 'Witness credibility assessment' ], legalIssues: [ 'Bail application procedures under CrPC', 'Burden of proof in criminal cases', 'Circumstantial evidence evaluation', 'Right to legal representation', 'Presumption of innocence' ] }; const getDifficultyIcon = (difficulty: string) => { switch (difficulty) { case 'Beginner': return '🌱'; case 'Intermediate': return '📊'; case 'Advanced': return '🔥'; case 'Expert': return '👑'; default: return '📚'; } }; return (
